summary.intent: Implement the 'Structured' compaction mode in a Rust chat compaction system, making it fully functional after it had been stubbed out as 'not yet implemented'.
summary.outcome: Structured mode was fully implemented with happy path, fallback, and iterative behaviors; 4 new tests added (215 chat tests total); all clippy, fmt, and feature configuration checks pass; committed and issue closed.

repo: Compaction modes (RecencyPreserving, Structured, LLM-replace) live in crates/chat/src/compaction_run.rs and are dispatched via a match on mode enum.
Boundary computation and post-splice cleanup logic was shared between RecencyPreserving and Structured by extracting helper functions.
Structured mode previously had a validation warning in crates/config/src/validate.rs marking it as unimplemented — this guard was removed on completion.
Documentation for compaction modes lives in docs/src/compaction.md; rustdoc for template/schema in crates/config/src/template.rs and crates/config/src/schema.rs.
The project uses a stub-provider pattern for LLM-dependent tests in compaction_run.rs.
Feature flag `--no-default-features` is a required CI check; `extract_previous_summary` needed a cfg attribute to suppress dead-code warnings under that configuration.

code.finding: Boundary computation and assembly were refactored into reusable helpers shared by recency_preserving_strategy and the new structured_strategy.
Contained a 'not yet implemented' guard for Structured mode that was removed once the implementation shipped.
extract_previous_summary triggered a dead-code warning under --no-default-features and required a conditional compilation attribute.

workflow: Issue claiming and closing are managed via Bash scripts integrated into the commit workflow.
The full green check sequence is: clippy → fmt → tests → no-default-features compile → commit.
fmt issues required multiple iterative fix-and-recheck cycles before the final clean state.
Test counts were verified explicitly after implementation to confirm the expected delta (+4 structured tests).

friction: Multiple fmt fix-recheck cycles were needed — fmt issues were not caught until after the initial compile and test pass.
dead-code warning for extract_previous_summary under --no-default-features required a separate cleanup pass after the main implementation was complete.
Two compile errors required edits after the initial implementation before tests could run.
